rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the target component type scope when the contract relates to component type features
Place AGREE annex blocks in the target implementation scope when the contract relates to implementation-specific bindings
Preserve existing AADL architecture by only inserting annex blocks, not modifying component definitions
Use visible identifiers from the current case when available, otherwise rely on case-provided bounds or relations
generic_rules: Insert annex blocks at the appropriate scope level to maintain architectural integrity
Preserve existing component definitions and connections when inserting annex blocks
Use visible identifiers from the current case when available for contract expressions
Avoid using subcomponent paths in component type annex blocks
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};`
Use `assume` and `guarantee` for property declarations, `eq` for equations, and `assign` for bindings
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type annexes when using concrete bounds or visible relations
anti_patterns: Do not use AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
Avoid placing subcomponent port references in component type annex blocks
Do not modify existing component definitions or connections when inserting annex blocks
